---
title: "02 Overfitting & Cross-Validation"
---

# 🎲 Random

- [landmark.am](https://landmark.am/)

![image.png](../00_random_image/02_ancient_churches_Armenian.jpg)


# 📚 Նյութը

- [📺 [06] Մոդելի գնահատում. overfitting ու cross validation](https://youtu.be/1j_xYLQrUAg), [🎞️ Սլայդեր](04_overfitting_cross_validation.pdf), [📝 Նշումներով](04_overfitting_cross_validation_notes.pdf)

📝 **Թեմայի վերաբերյալ հարցաշար (Google Form):** TBD

***

# 🏡 Տնային

## Project — Predicting apartment rent, *done right* 🧀🧀🧀

This project **continues HW2**: same `House_Rent_Dataset.csv`, same target (`Rent`).
Last time you fit a plain linear regression. Now the goal is an **honest, well-tuned
model** that uses everything from this chapter — cross-validation, regularization,
hyperparameter tuning, and the right error metric.

It is **open-ended**: you choose the modeling approach. The rubric below defines what a
good submission looks like. Slides to lean on: `04_overfitting_cross_validation.pdf`,
`05_regularization.pdf`, `06_hyperparameter_tuning.pdf`, `07_regression_metrics.pdf`.

### Setup

- **Data:** the same file as HW2 — `House_Rent_Dataset.csv` (4,746 rows), in `ml_new/01_regression__main_concepts/data/` (from a notebook in this folder: `../01_regression__main_concepts/data/House_Rent_Dataset.csv`).
- **Target:** `Rent` (monthly rent, INR).
- **Deliverable:** one reproducible notebook (`rent_project.ipynb`) that ends with a short written conclusion (3–5 sentences).
- **Seed:** use `509` wherever a seed is needed.

Do **not** spend the evening on preprocessing. A reasonable minimal setup:

- Drop `Posted On`, `Area Locality` (2,000+ unique values), and `Point of Contact`.
- One-hot encode `City`, `Furnishing Status`, `Area Type`, `Tenant Preferred`.
- Keep `BHK`, `Size`, `Bathroom` numeric.
- Put scaling/encoding **inside** a `Pipeline` so they run within each CV fold (no leakage).
- `Floor` (e.g. "1 out of 3") is messy — parsing it is a **bonus**, not required.

### What you must accomplish

You are free in *how*, but the submission must show all of:

1. **An honest evaluation protocol.** Hold out a test set up front; use k-fold cross-validation on the rest. The test set is touched **once**, at the very end.
2. **Overfitting, shown and controlled.** Heads-up: with 4,746 rows and few real features (only `Size` is truly continuous) this dataset will *not* overfit on its own — you have to push it there. Build a high-variance model (high-degree `PolynomialFeatures` *with* interactions, or train on a small subsample of a few hundred rows) and show train error falling while validation error climbs. Then bring it back under control with cross-validation.
3. **Regularization, with interpretation.** Add a penalty to the *same* high-variance model from step 2 — Ridge, Lasso, ElasticNet. (Regularizing a model that doesn't overfit looks like it does nothing — that's exactly why step 2 comes first.) Show coefficient paths vs the penalty strength and explain *what changes*: which features Lasso drives to zero, and whether that makes sense for rent. Expanding only the numeric columns via `ColumnTransformer` keeps this tidy — polynomial products of the one-hot dummies are mostly noise.
4. **Hyperparameter tuning, ≥ 2 strategies.** Tune with at least two of `GridSearchCV`, `RandomizedSearchCV`, Optuna. Compare what each found and at what budget.
5. **A justified metric.** `Rent` is heavily right-skewed (a few luxury flats dominate). Pick the metric(s) you optimize and report, and **justify** it — contrast RMSE vs MAE vs R² vs MAPE; consider a `log(Rent)` target or Huber loss.
6. **A single honest final number.** Report test-set performance once, with a short conclusion: which model, which features mattered, what you'd try with more time.

### Rules

- No test-set peeking before the final step.
- Reproducible top to bottom (fixed seed, runs end-to-end without manual fiddling).
- Scaling/encoding live inside the pipeline, never applied to the full dataset before splitting.

### Bonus (up to +10)

- `TransformedTargetRegressor` with a log target, compared fairly to the untransformed model.
- **Quantile regression** to output a rent *range* (P10–P90), not just a point estimate.
- Optuna over a wider space (penalty strength + `l1_ratio` + polynomial degree).
- Per-city error analysis: where is the model worst, and why?
- Parse `Floor` into numeric features and measure whether it helps.

### Grading rubric (100 pts)

| Criterion | Points |
|---|---|
| Honest evaluation protocol, no leakage | 20 |
| Overfitting demonstrated and controlled (CV) | 15 |
| Regularization (Ridge / Lasso / ElasticNet) + interpretation | 20 |
| Hyperparameter tuning (≥ 2 strategies, compared) | 15 |
| Metric choice justified for the skewed target | 15 |
| Final test evaluation + written conclusions | 10 |
| Code quality & reproducibility | 5 |
| Bonus | up to +10 |

### Hints / common traps

- Lasso and Ridge **need scaled features** — unscaled, the penalty unfairly hits large-unit columns like `Size`.
- Run scaling/encoding **inside** cross-validation, not before splitting, or your CV scores are optimistic.
- **Inspect the tail before trusting RMSE:** one listing is 3,500,000 (the next is 1,200,000) and `Rent` has skew ≈ 21 — a couple of points dominate squared error. Handle them deliberately; don't silently drop them.
- If you care about the *typical* tenant, MAE (or a `log(Rent)` target) tells a different story than RMSE. Decide *whose* error you minimize.
- "Best CV score" ≠ "best test score" — don't tune on the test set.


<a href="http://s01.flagcounter.com/more/1oO"><img src="https://s01.flagcounter.com/count2/1oO/bg_FFFFFF/txt_000000/border_CCCCCC/columns_2/maxflags_10/viewers_0/labels_0/pageviews_1/flags_0/percent_0/" alt="Flag Counter"></a>
